# License Plates and Vulgarity: A Tool

## By Naomi Liftman, Eva Reutercrona, Oviya Muralidharan

Background: Vanity license plates are a fun and simple way to add style
and character to your car. In California when you apply for a vanity
license plate a person at the DMV manually checks if your plate contains
vulgar or offensive language.

Questions of Interest: How are they already rejecting? And what their
reasoning is? Check Twitter for anything they missed?

Data Sources:

-   <https://github.com/veltman/ca-license-plates/tree/master>
    (California License Plates)

-   <https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/muhammadatef/english-profanity-words-dataset>
    (English Profanity)

-   <https://github.com/LDNOOBW/List-of-Dirty-Naughty-Obscene-and-Otherwise-Bad-Words/blob/master/en>
    (list of naughty words)

-   <https://www.cs.cmu.edu/~biglou/resources/bad-words.txt> (bad word
    txt)

-   <https://github.com/4troDev/profanity.csv/blob/main/English.csv>
    (swears in English)

-   <https://itg.nls.uk/wiki/Contemporary_Slurs> (ethnic slurs in usable
    csv)

Goal for project outcome: A tool that the state of California could use
to identify if plates should be rejected or accepted.
